ARGUMENTS:
{"idea": {"Name": "geometric_interference_cancellation", "Title": "Interference-Aware Latent Steering: Zero-Training Test-Time Optimization via Geometric Gradient Cancellation", "Short Hypothesis": "Test-time compute scaling implicitly optimizes a latent reasoning landscape, but sequential refinement steps often traverse conflicting gradient directions, causing destructive interference that inflates hidden-state perturbation without improving accuracy. We hypothesize that explicitly identifying and projecting out these anti-aligned latent directions via a training-free, covariance-conditioned geometric cancellation mechanism will strictly bound wall-clock latency, minimize perturbation costs, and yield Pareto-optimal throughput-accuracy frontiers. This direction is necessary because current methods rely on opaque heuristics or external verifiers that add latency and fail to mechanistically address latent conflict.
